page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized and storage-equipped server instance. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The system is configured with 372 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. Local storage consists of 2951 GB of NVMe SSD capacity distributed across 8 local SSDs. No dedicated GPU hardware is included.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core stress-ng performance and Geekbench single-core scores rank in the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads placing in the top 25%, though large-block write bandwidth drops to the middle 50%. Computational tasks such as gzip compression and image processing achieve top-tier performance. For database operations, the server registers strong results, including top 25% performance in Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations.

While the server excels in memory-intensive tasks and local storage operations, its multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng sits in the middle 50%, representing a potential tradeoff for highly parallelized compute-bound workloads. Additionally, large-scale LLM inference using 70B models drops to average performance, though smaller model inference remains strong. The hardware configuration is suited for memory-intensive databases, local caching layers, software compilation, and data processing tasks requiring high local storage throughput and substantial memory capacity.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server configured with 48 vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. It features 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives, with no GPU accelerators. The server delivers strong performance across multiple benchmarks, with single-core processing, image processing, and gzip compression reaching the top 10% to 25% of tested systems. Memory read bandwidth is highly efficient, though large-block write bandwidth and multi-core stress-ng performance sit in the middle 50%. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from fast local storage. It is suited for database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd features 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and 2951 GB NVMe SSD storage. It delivers strong single-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48-lssd is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 372 GB RAM, and 2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 platform featuring 372 GB RAM and 2.9 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
372 GB of system memory providing 7.75 GB per core
2951 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 8 drives
Top-tier single-core execution and image processing speeds
Strong database performance and high-bandwidth memory reads
Suited for memory-intensive databases and software compilation
